steps.title: Identify the task goal
steps.content: The question asks which sentence emphasizes the scope of Thompson’s work — meaning how broad or large his artistic output was. The key note is: “more than two thousand drawings and paintings.” A choice that uses this total-output figure to frame a single work (making it look small by comparison) directly conveys scope.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Mater* is just one of more than two thousand drawings and paintings completed by American artist Malik Thompson.: Uses “just one of more than two thousand” to contrast a single piece against the full body of work, directly emphasizing scope. ✓
  • Choice B — Though artist Malik Thompson completed most of his two thousand-plus drawings in the 1980s, his work *Mater* is composed of acrylic and oil stick on canvas.: Mentions the count in a subordinate clause but pivots to medium/materials (“acrylic and oil stick”) as the main point — emphasizing medium, not scope. ✗
  • Choice C — At a 2009 auction, artist Malik Thompson's *Mater*, composed of acrylic and oil stick on canvas, sold for \$5.8 million.: Focuses on sale price; omits any mention of the total output. ✗
  • Choice D — Decades after artist Malik Thompson completed his 1982 work *Mater*, a private collection purchased it for \$5.8 million.: Focuses on timing and auction price; says nothing about the breadth of his output. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
